tests.title: Correct build under test
tests.requires: You installed the release intended for this test pass.
tests.expected: The control panel version banner shows the build under test (0.10.1), so every result maps to the right code. A stale install must never masquerade as a bug.

steps.text: Open the control panel (http://127.0.0.1:8765) or the packaged app window.
Confirm the version banner reads 0.10.1 - it must match the installer you just ran.
If it does NOT match, STOP and install the correct build first - results from a stale build are invalid.

tests.title: Landing-gear keybind: arm, separate-turn confirm, guards, abort
tests.requires: `[elite].enabled` and `[keybinds].enabled = true`, `require_confirmation`/`combat_guard`/`mode_guard = true`. Toggle Landing Gear bound in ED (on the `binding_preference` slot, default Primary). Do this parked/docked and safe.
tests.expected: Toggling gear arms without firing, requires a confirm on a SEPARATE turn, refuses same-turn confirm, refuses in danger or with ED off, expires after the window, hard-aborts on "belay that", refuses any off-allowlist control, is mode-gated (refused/not offered on foot, re-checked at confirm), and presses the key from the configured binding slot.

steps.text: Arm: "COVAS, toggle my landing gear." → says armed-but-not-done and asks to confirm separately. Gear does NOT move yet.
Confirm on a SEPARATE turn: "Confirm." (or "do it") → the gear toggles in-game.
Same-turn confirm refused: arm and, in the same utterance, say "…and do it now" → refuses to fire in the arming turn.
Combat guard: get interdicted / into danger, then ask to toggle → refuses. With `[elite]` OFF it also refuses.
Expiry: arm, wait past `confirm_window` (60 s), then "confirm" → says it expired; nothing fires.
Hard abort: arm, then "Abort." / "Belay that." → arm cleared, any held key released.
Off-allowlist refusal: ask for a different control ("deploy hardpoints") → won't do it.
Mode gate on foot (#29): disembark (on foot) and ask to toggle landing gear → refuses ("only works in your ship") and doesn't offer the action. Back in the ship, the same request arms normally.
Mode re-check at confirm (#29): in the ship, arm the toggle; before confirming, disembark; then "confirm" → refused (mode re-checked at confirm time), nothing fires.

Binding preference (#29): with the keyboard key on Primary (normal), startup log shows `landing_gear -> <Key>` and it presses that. Set `[keybinds].binding_preference = "secondary"` with the key on the Secondary slot → it uses that instead (falls back to the other slot if only one is bound).

tests.title: Tier-1 ship-systems toggles: fire-immediately, allowlist-gated, pips, combat guard, mode gate (issue #31)
tests.requires: `[keybinds].enabled` + `[elite].enabled` on. Benign main-ship toggles that fire immediately (no arm/confirm). Off until you add each macro NAME to `[keybinds].allowlist`; bind the matching control to a KEY in ED. Do these parked/docked. Names: cargo_scoop, night_vision, ship_lights, hud_mode, pips_engines/weapons/systems/balance.
tests.expected: An allowlisted toggle fires immediately (no confirm); a non-allowlisted one is refused; pips move per request and balance resets to 2/2/2; HUD mode flips combat<->analysis; the combat guard still refuses benign toggles in danger; on-foot is mode-gated; an unbound control asks you to bind a key.

steps.text: Opt-in fires immediately: add `cargo_scoop` to the allowlist, then 'toggle my cargo scoop' -> the scoop deploys/retracts right away (no 'armed, confirm separately' step). Startup log lists `cargo_scoop -> <Key>`.
Not allowlisted -> refused: with `ship_lights` NOT in the allowlist, 'turn on my ship lights' -> won't do it (off-allowlist), nothing presses.
Pips: allowlist `pips_engines`, say 'pips to engines' three times -> three pips move into ENG. Then allowlist + say 'balance the pips' (`pips_balance`) -> distribution resets to 2/2/2.
HUD mode: allowlist `hud_mode`, 'switch HUD to analysis mode' -> the HUD flips combat<->analysis.
Combat guard still applies: with a benign toggle allowlisted, get interdicted / into danger and ask for it -> refuses (benign toggles aren't exempt).
Mode gating: disembark (on foot) and ask for cargo scoop -> refuses ('only works in your ship') and isn't offered. Back in the ship it fires.
Unbound control: if the matching ED control is on a HOTAS/mouse only (no keyboard bind), asking for it -> 'bind it to a key' message; nothing fires.

tests.title: Tier-1 flight / nav actions: benign fire-now vs arm-and-confirm, targeting, combat + mode gates (issue #30)
tests.requires: Off by default. For each action add its name to `[keybinds].allowlist` AND bind the matching control to a KEY in ED. `[keybinds].enabled` + `[elite].enabled` on. Do parked/docked first, then open space with a clear area. Combat guard + mode gate apply to every one.
tests.expected: Benign actions (throttle_*, target cycling, target_next_route_system) fire immediately; consequential ones (supercruise, frame_shift_drive, hyperspace, flight_assist) arm and require a separate confirm; combat/danger and ED-off refuse; a deployed fighter is mode-gated off the main-ship-only actions; an unbound token says to bind it; an off-allowlist action is refused.

steps.text: Benign fires immediately: allowlist `throttle_zero`; 'COVAS, cut the throttle.' -> throttle drops to zero at once (no separate confirm), reply 'Throttle at zero'. Same for `throttle_50` / `throttle_100`.
Targeting (benign): allowlist `cycle_next_target` + `select_target_ahead`; 'target the ship ahead' then 'cycle to the next target' -> target reticle changes immediately each time.
Route target (benign): with a route plotted, allowlist `target_next_route_system`; 'target the next system in my route' -> the next route system is selected immediately.

Consequential arms-and-confirms: allowlist `supercruise`; 'engage supercruise' -> armed but not done; on a separate 'confirm' it fires. Same shape for `frame_shift_drive`, `hyperspace`, and `flight_assist`.
Combat guard: in danger/interdiction, any flight action refuses; with `[elite]` OFF it refuses too.

Mode gate - fighter: deploy a ship-launched fighter; `throttle_*` and target cycling are still offered, but `supercruise` / `hyperspace` / `frame_shift_drive` / `target_next_route_system` / `nav_lock` are NOT (main-ship only) and refuse if asked.
Unbound token: allowlist `nav_lock` but leave WingNavLock unbound in ED -> asking to toggle nav lock says to bind it in-game; nothing fires.
Off-allowlist still refused: an action you did NOT add (e.g. `hyperspace` when only `throttle_zero` is allowlisted) -> won't do it.

tests.title: Odyssey on-foot actions: mode-gated to foot, benign fire-now, weapon select/holster, combat guard (issue #34)
tests.requires: Disembark first (on foot in Odyssey). `[keybinds].enabled` on. Add the macros under test to `[keybinds].allowlist` (e.g. on_foot_flashlight, on_foot_night_vision). Bind the matching On Foot controls to keys in ED. These are benign - they fire immediately.
tests.expected: On-foot actions are offered only on foot (refused/hidden while flying) and fire immediately there; flashlight/night vision/weapon-select+holster/suit-tools/crouch/galaxy-map all work; it never FIRES a weapon; the combat guard still refuses benign on-foot actions in danger; off-allowlist is refused; a ship control (landing gear) is refused while on foot (gate proven both ways).

steps.text: Mode gating - offered only on foot: in your ship, ask to 'toggle my flashlight' -> refused ('only works on foot'), and not offered. Disembark, ask again -> it fires immediately. This is the core check: on-foot actions are hidden while flying.
Flashlight / night vision: on foot, 'flashlight' and 'night vision' each toggle the suit light / night vision.
Weapon select + holster: on foot, 'draw your primary weapon' / 'secondary' / 'utility' selects that weapon; 'holster your weapon' puts it away. It never FIRES - only draws/holsters.
Suit tools: on foot, 'switch to your energy link' / 'profile analyser' / 'suit tool' selects that gadget.
Crouch / galaxy map: on foot, 'crouch' and 'open the galaxy map' work.
Combat guard on foot: get into danger on foot, ask to toggle flashlight -> refused (benign still guarded). With `[elite]` OFF it also refuses.
Off-allowlist refusal: ask for an on-foot macro you did NOT add to the allowlist -> won't do it.
Ship control hidden on foot (regression): while on foot, 'toggle landing gear' -> refused and not offered (proves the gate both ways).

tests.title: SRV / buggy controls: benign fire-now, recall arm-and-confirm, mode gate, combat guard (issue #35)
tests.requires: `[keybinds].enabled` on. Add the SRV macros you want to `[keybinds].allowlist` (drive_assist, srv_headlights, srv_night_vision, srv_cargo_scoop, srv_auto_brake, recall_ship). Bind the matching Buggy controls to keys in ED. Deploy the SRV first - these are offered ONLY while driving.
tests.expected: Benign SRV toggles fire immediately while driving; recall_ship arms and needs a separate confirm (same-turn refused); back in the ship/on foot the SRV actions are refused/not offered; disembarking the SRV after arming recall re-checks the mode at confirm and refuses; the combat guard and ED-off refuse; off-allowlist and weapons/turret are never offered; an unbound control asks to bind a key.

steps.text: Benign toggle fires immediately (in SRV): while driving the SRV, 'COVAS, turn on the headlights.' -> headlights toggle right away (no confirm); same for 'toggle drive assist', 'night vision', 'cargo scoop', 'auto-brake'. Log shows e.g. `executed srv_headlights -> <Key>`.
Recall ship arms-and-confirms: in the SRV, 'recall my ship.' -> armed but not done; confirm on a separate turn ('confirm') -> the ship recall/dismiss fires. Same-turn confirm is refused.
Mode gating - not in the SRV: back in the main ship (or on foot), ask for any SRV control ('headlights', 'recall my ship') -> refused ('only works in the SRV') and not offered.
Mode gating - exit SRV after arming recall: in the SRV, arm `recall_ship`; before confirming, board your ship; then 'confirm' -> refused (mode re-checked at confirm), nothing fires.
Combat guard: in the SRV, get into danger, then ask for any SRV toggle -> refuses. With `[elite]` OFF it also refuses.
Off-allowlist refusal: ask for an SRV control you did NOT allowlist -> won't do it. Weapons/turret are never offered.
Unbound control: if a Buggy control (e.g. Night Vision) is HOTAS/mouse-only -> COVAS says to bind it to a key; nothing fires.

tests.title: Tier-1 UI actions: panels / maps / fire groups fire immediately, allowlist + combat + mode gates (issue #32)
tests.requires: Benign fire-immediately actions (no confirm). Opt in by NAME in `[keybinds].allowlist` (e.g. open_galaxy_map, cycle_fire_group_next, focus_left_panel). Each ED control must be bound to a key. `[keybinds].enabled` + `[elite].enabled` on. Do first tests parked and docked.
tests.expected: Allowlisted UI actions fire on the spoken command (no confirm) - galaxy/system map, HUD panels, fire-group cycling, UI back/focus, head-look; a non-allowlisted macro is refused; the combat guard refuses benign UI actions in danger; on-foot is mode-gated (fire-group cycling also works in a fighter); an unbound control reports 'bind it in-game'.

steps.text: Fires immediately (no confirm): with `open_galaxy_map` allowlisted, 'open the galaxy map' -> the map opens on the spoken command. Say it again to close.

Panels: allowlist a panel (e.g. `focus_left_panel`) -> 'open the navigation panel' focuses the correct HUD panel. Spot-check `focus_right_panel`, `focus_comms_panel`, `focus_role_panel`, `quick_comms`, `open_system_map`.
Fire groups: with `cycle_fire_group_next` / `cycle_fire_group_previous` allowlisted, 'next fire group' / 'previous fire group' steps the active fire group (top-right HUD).
UI / head-look: allowlist `ui_back`, `ui_focus`, `toggle_headlook` -> each presses the matching control.
Not allowlisted = refused: with a macro NOT in the allowlist, asking for it -> won't do it (even though the action exists).
Combat guard still applies: while interdicted / in danger, ask to open the galaxy map -> refused (benign actions are still gated).
Mode gating: on foot, ask to open the galaxy map / focus a panel -> refused ('only works in your ship'); fire-group cycling also works in a deployed fighter.
Unbound control: if the ED control is HOTAS/mouse-only (no keyboard bind), the action reports 'bind it in-game' and nothing fires. Startup log shows each allowlisted macro `-> <Key>`.
